reasoning: The informal statement asserts that -(-v) = v for every v in a vector space V. The Lean formalization sets up F as a Field, V as an AddCommGroup with a Module F V structure (the standard Mathlib encoding of "vector space V over field F"), and asserts -(-v) = v for an implicit v : V (giving universal quantification over V). The hypotheses, conclusion, quantifier structure, and types all match the informal statement exactly. While the property -(-v) = v actually holds in any additive group, the Lean version preserves the vector-space context of the informal statement without adding or dropping anything material.
